JV here. I'm here for your daily motivation slash skills training. I'm doing this as I'm walking to the beach. Great topic for today to start our week, right? I want to talk about something that came up in a podcast. There's three people on the other panel interviewing me. And one of them, one was a really famous young kid. The other two were just sort of support people. One of the support people brought up this point about how, you know, about the idea about acting as if, you know, act as if you're a wealthy man, rich already, you'll become rich. Act as if you have the answers and the answers will come to you. Act as if you have confidence, people will become confident. You act as if, right? And I always tell the story about how when I was a kid, I was watching TV and so what David Copperfield make the statute of liberty disappear, right? I was flipped out. I said, I want to be a magician, right? The first thing I did was I went out and I essentially put an ad in the local penny saver. It was called that was the local circulation back then, right? And I put in children's parties. The amazing Belford $25, right? Children's magic shows, right? Didn't think anyone would call and the phone starts ringing off the hook. Well, guess what? I didn't even know how to do magic and I started to panic, right? I went to my father and he took me into this and said, dad, that's a mistake, right? And he took me into the city, bought me some tricks, have great memories of that going to the shop called Louis Tannen, right? But the point is, is that I actually was running a really powerful strategy called acting as if. Now, I want you to understand that's not quite the right way to run that strategy. You don't want to like advertise for a product that you have no finger power and say you're an expert. But the flip side of the equation is that what a lot of you do, and it's not just starting a business, it's just doing, executing on anything. You wait until you're an expert to execute or to your really competent. And it's just, listen, you can't become an expert without going out into the real world and doing something. There is no perfect plan. There is no way of mastering a technique or an art without going down and tripping and falling a few times and looking clunky. You understand? Like, I, so this kid had actually started, advertised himself as a social media guru without knowing the first thing about it and got a hit from a very big company, right? And he went in and he faked it till he made it. And what he did do though, and why it didn't turn into a disaster, is that he then quickly closed the knowledge gap. He began studying like men, hooked himself with the right people and became one of the top people in social media marketing. You get it now. The idea is that what makes this work and what doesn't make it work is the fact that you must close the knowledge gap or not. If you don't, you can't go out there putting yourself out. I'm an expert. I'm grabbing the top guy like this. And then once you start getting traction, you're not studying and learning like men and growing yourself like man. So you actually become a real expert. You can't fake it till you make it for very long. It's a very short period of time. You should have at least some knowledge under your belt. You don't want to go to the stone cold novice with knowing nothing about what you're doing. That's dangerous unless you're a really good talker. It's just not necessary. You can get yourself up to speed enough, become sort of a dabbler and a dilettante. You know a little bit to talk about it and then quickly become an expert. What you don't want to do though, what you don't want to do is sit there and just wait, wait, wait, wait, wait, keep educating yourself, growing yourself and never take action because you don't feel like you have enough success under your belt, enough experience under your belt to feel comfortable branding yourself as an expert. The only way to become an expert is on the job. By the way, and the truth is, and I think that you probably know that. If you're guilty of this stuff, and by the way, there's degrees. Some people are just terrible and they never move off the mark. There are others probably many more you who only have a guilty little bit. You probably should take action a bit more robustly, a bit earlier. You eventually do get off the mark but you delay everything in your life. You delay the manifestation of the things that you want because you're always slightly behind in your action taken. I want you to really take a close look at that this week really and start fucking putting yourself out there, branding yourself, position yourself as an expert. Don't settle for anything less. It's the way you can. Remember. This is all on an unconscious level and so on. Think you're an expert. Proceeds you that way. You're matching a pattern that they have that what experts look like, what experts sound like, how they act, how they carry themselves, the words they say. But in the beginning, it's not so much about the words. Obviously, there are some industry buzzwords you want to look. That's why I said you don't have to go in a little bit. But the point is it's mostly the unconscious communication that carries the day. You don't have to wait to become a true expert to put yourself out there as an expert as long as you know enough. And you're because there's no way to become an expert without putting yourself out there. There's no other way. So there's two paths in life. There's the path of like the slow, slog, burn a 10 fucking watt light bulb your whole life and never really go out there and just put yourself out there. And by the way, make the consequences of failure so dire and unthinkable that you won't let it happen. Like put yourself out there. Like you say, God, if I fail, I'll be so fucking embarrassed. It will be a dessert. Don't let you get it. Once you do that, it's almost like your brain won't let you fail. You'll do the work. You'll study. You'll hook up with the right people. You'll close that knowledge gap. And once you do, once you get to the habit of everything starts to accelerate in your life. I'm talking about sales, entrepreneurship, becoming a top producer, getting referrals, everything. Everything starts to accelerate. You've applied this technique in all aspects of your life starting now. And I'm sure again, I'm sure very few of you were on that really, really slow slog. You probably wouldn't be here if you were on that slow slog where you never take action because you took action to sign up for this program, right? But you're probably somewhere like, you know, in the middle, maybe 60, 70%. You're not quite where you're really putting yourself out there. You're just fucking, you're after that as if at the highest level and working your ass off to close the knowledge gap and you live an incredible life as a result. Love you all.
